reviews.review_description: Very poor implementation of an idea. Waste of money, deleting!
Confusing directions, not sure how to move through the chapters and the Community website does not com up.

reviews.review_title: I'm new to Vr the reviews don't lie
reviews.review_description: So I'm super new to Vr but totally obsessed I can't stop entering new worlds and exploring different games so much fun!! I discovered this app and honestly it is my favorite so far. There's not a whole lot to it as in graphics or activity but the getting to hear other peoples answers from all different walks a life is so exciting and just like everyone else has admitted answering at least on if the questions I broke down and cried it honestly was touching. If your a touchy feely kind of person this app/game is for you!!

This game was so heartfelt and beautiful. It had gorgeous environments and listening to people's stories and perspectives on things was just so sweet. Some people had wonderful stories about love thats lasted 20+ years, other people talked about how they overcame their circumstances to achieve things. It was such a wonderful experience. I would love to play more, with more prompts. It did have me crying at some points but that just shows what a touching game this is.

Very misleading, I purchased this app 20 minutes ago in the hope of getting an alternative to Tripp. There are no graphics /calming music to enjoy. Just a load of silly questions, am I missing the point here???

Stressful in a good way of course, for people like me even thinking about your emotions is nerve-wracking. This game made me feel comfortable enough to listen and even record my own thoughts. There’s an odd comfort to opening up to others when they’ll never identify you. 

My complaints consist of wanting more questions added and potentially an option for people to connect to one another anonymously in-game? There were times where i was hearing people’s stories where i wanted to jump in, but that isn’t the meaning of this game i suppose.
This game gave a whole new meaning to emotional. Listening to other peoples stories and their emotions really made me feel like I wasn’t alone. Each voice and person was so unique.

Look I get the significance for this type of experience, and I'm thankful that it is a powerful and touching journey for a lot of people who maybe needed to hear some of these things, but for those who may not be in that head space, this may not be for you. I'm not taking anything away from all of the 4-5 star reviews, it deserves that if it connects with the user that way, but I think it's also important to keep expectations realistic for those going into it expecting more. It's a very simple journey, where you listen to other user's recordings, and although they are from the heart and mean a lot to those who voiced them, after a few minutes it becomes fairly generic and predictable. These aren't paid, experienced writers like dialog usually consists of. So if you could use some confirmation or uplifting inspiration, then by all means, jump in here and you will love it. But if you're not in that mindset at this point in your life, this will probably be a disappointing experience for you.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your feedback! Yeah, this is definitely an experience more geared towards people who are into VR, intimacy and exploring the minds of others :)

reviews.review_description: I find the interface odd and confusing. I often don’t know what I’m supposed to do next. The concept is great, but ive entered four answers. Two recorded fine. One recorded intermittently. And the final one was a deeply heartfelt answer about a friend i missed. Unfortunately i never saw any warning about a time limit. Nothing told me to stop talking, so i poured my heart into this only to find that only the first few words were recorded. Wish to hell i had gotten a refund. I uninstalled it. Quite angry at the waste of time and emotion. Really a stupid waste of time. Quite angry. Tell the speaker when their time is up! Give them a count down bar. Something! For God’s sake, don’t waste people’s time like this. This is what puts people off of tech. It seldom, seldom works. Really angry. How many more emotional answers have you silently truncated!?

developer_response.body: I'm going to make improvements on the next update. Thanks for the constructive criticism and the purchase! :)

reviews.review_description: Bought it and I don’t regret it. What an incredible experience! If you’re into VR for its ability to connect you to people and the unique experiences that the platform provides, this is a very compelling ‘game’. It’s so intimate. While not for everyone, this is one of my personal favorite ‘must have’ titles. It’s beautiful.

You are asked a question, and you can hear the answers left by other users, before you can leave your own. You listen to these sound bites by touching one of around 10 to 20 bubble faces meant to represent users. The answer you leave can be 'found' by other users. This is the theory behind the experience , if I understand correctly. 

In practice, the voices seem scripted and recorded in the same professional environment. The accents are all very similar, so is the age-bracket of the people doing the voices. The age of the actor and the content are also often mismatched, making the experience more like a college class project than the output of a genuine audience.

I found no way of discovering more than the 10 to 20 odd soundbites for each question. All similarly styled and voiced. Where are the real user sound bites?  How do the devs filter out profanity and poor quality sound bites left by users? Who gets to choose? How are they managing to keep up as the user base grows? The model seems totally non-scalable for a small company, which is why this feels more like a scam than a genuine experience. 

Also, there is absolutely no useful use of VR. This could have just as easily been a phone App, and not a good one at that. I am confused.

developer_response.body: Hi, Maltese! All messages in this experience are user-submitted and there was no scripting involved. Many of them sound really good because they were recorded with the Oculus Rift CV1, which has an amazing directional mic. That being said, I pushed an update today that fixed a bug that caused some of the recordings to repeat, so it should all be good to go! Personally I believe that listening to those stories (and sharing them) in this immersive environment changes things completely, but to each their own :)

reviews.review_description: This is not a game so don’t buy it if that’s what you are looking for. However if you are looking for a truly emotional VR experience then this might be the app for you. In this experience you are given a simple question. There are currently five. None really that personal but questions we can all answer because of our life’s experiences. You listen to anonymously recorded voices tell you their dreams and hopes and so much more. It’s an experiment in the human condition and may cause you to feel many emotions.

reviews.review_title: Deep Sharing in Beautiful Surreal Scenes
reviews.review_description: I love the soothing, surreal/dreamlike low poly environments, and having these glimpses into other people's lives -- and being given the opportunity to anonymously share my own answers to the provocative questions -- is wonderfully heart-opening. I also have this on SteamVR. I was happy to pay for it again for the Quest, both because I prefer it on a tetherless VR headset, and to support the developer and encourage more innovative indie VR experiences like this

Although my initial pass at this experience went by pretty quickly, i can see myself returning to this world in the future to listen to new responses and maybe to update my own.  It’s such thought provoking concept - a really innovative use of VR! Also I was pleasantly surprised at how honest these strangers had been and instantly felt like i belonged to a little therapy circle. 

I can’t wait for more content and hope they add additional prompts/chapters soon! Thank you!!
You can never know where someone comes from,what they experience, how they overcome it..but this experience is really eye opening and so glad i sign on to the beta version thank you Lucas Rizzotto

The questions the devs of this platform asks you are simple but it makes you stop and think about yourself and your life. It took awhile for me to answer "describe the person you miss", that one hit me quite hard. 

Some answers made me shed quite a few tears and I actually hugged their orbs, but I made sure I listened to every single orb around me because it does feel really personal in that moment hear what that person answered.

reviews.review_title: You need to show this to everyone
reviews.review_description: Title says it all. One of the most well designed, impactful VR experiences to date. I don’t usually do the whole review thing, but this justifies it.

You listen to others stories and get to leave your own and surprisingly after I finished the 5 chapters I felt relieved somehow like a weight was taken off my shoulders. Shows you what telling strangers can do.

reviews.review_title: Listening to the same stories in order
reviews.review_description: I love the game itself the idea is amazing but i find when i log in after.pmaying the firstntime i have to listen 2 the same stories i heard again before i hear any new ones so they are all in the same order. I want to hear more variett of stories.

developer_response.body: Hey, inhaylexhay! There was a bug that was causing the same recordings to be replayed over and over. I just released an update today that fixes it. Sorry about that!

reviews.review_description: Maybe for some people this would be very meaningful. The best part about this game is the introspection you are guided through. Not really worth the paid VR experience for me
